stories.headline: Hitching a Free Ride
stories.summary: An examination of nonprofit organizations in the U.S. reveals significant tax advantages and financial power while they compete with for-profit enterprises. Reporters explore how nonprofits manage 1.475 trillion in assets while maintaining tax-exempt status.

stories.headline: When agencies don't deliver records
stories.summary: Courts and government agencies are divided on interpretations of public records laws, with some using technical means like computer tape formats to obstruct media and public access to government information.

stories.headline: IRE wins grant to start operation
stories.summary: Investigative Reporters and Editors (IRE) received a $544,000 three-year grant to support investigative journalism efforts across the Americas.

stories.headline: Tracking nonprofits
stories.summary: The Internet provides resources for journalists to research and background non-profit organizations, with references to specific gopher servers and databases that can assist in this reporting process.
